rulings.Rulings: Hearing appearance required. Remote appearance by video or telephone should be made whenever possible. Please review the 10th amended general order for more information. To appear by Zoom, go to Zoom.com and click Join Meeting, and use the ID and password below. Meeting ID: 875 8017 7925 Password: 722809 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. ISSUES: 1) Court to determine how to proceed. 2) Issue in PES-16-299969 Paul Perdue as attorney for Jack Jair has not provided the required proposed order for the status petition. Petitioner must submit a hard copy of the proposed order to the Examiner in room 202 at least five (5) court days in advance of the scheduled hearing date, with the scheduled hearing date noted on the face sheet. [See LRSF 14.7].

rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF DANIEL M. POMMON JR.
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #8, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 12/7/21. No courtesy copy of the objection filed on 10/20/21 has been received yet by the assigned Examiner as of 11/5/21. [LRSF 2.7 B. and 14.7 B. and C.] If the conservator seeks an accounting by the trustee, that must be sought in the trust case. The relief is also missing from her petition caption in any event. [CRC 7.102.] The accounting has not been fully reviewed yet; however, it is unclear why assets on hand at the end of the prior accounting showed a Chase CD with a carry value but no market value, when CDs are essentially cash. Courtesy copies must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 11/23/21 to be considered.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF AJAY KODURI
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting, Report, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. The administrator continued to use the estate funds to make mortgage payments for the decedent's condominium in Virginia. The administrator recharacterizes those payments as constructive distributions for the benefit of the surviving spouse to whom the Virginia condominium passed without ancillary administration in Virginia. rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF PUI LIN HUEY
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: continued to 11/23/21. no later than noon on 11/16/21, counsel must provide a revised order that has Order for Final Distribution first in the caption and orders after discovered property to the trustee of the trust. in addition, the petition calculates the statutory fees on a lower amount than the value of the I & A. if increased fees will be requested, that request must be in an amendment to the petition. a supplement(signed and verified by the petitioner) must be filed that explains why the petition says the fees will be paid from the estate as funds become available, and the order says fees shall be paid from the estate, but the petition says the estate holds only real property and minimal tangible personal property. the supplement must also address the additional language re when notice is required under Probate Code section 9202(b).

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE WILLIAM MICHAEL HENRY REVOCABLE TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For An Order To Confirm Validity Of Trust; Confirm Trustee; And Confirm Trustee; And Confirm Assets To Revocable Trust
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required on the sole issue of whether this petition should be taken off calendar for lack of progress. The petition has been pending for more than a year since the initial hearing in October 2020, with 8 continuances and 3 appearances. There are at least 17 first cousins once removed who would be the heirs and who must be served in the same manner required for a summons in a civil action , but only 11 of them have been served, according to the proofs of service on file. No additional proof of service on the following 6 heirs has been filed: Timothy James Smith; Darlene (Brown) Hale; Matthew T. Smith; Rebecca A. (Smith) Oden; Thomas J. Tyndell; and Daniel W. Tyndell. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 875 8017 7925 and password 722809. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and key in meeting ID "875 8017 7925#", then participant ID "0#", then password "722809#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appear by video and that they input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF CARY CALLEWAERT
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 12/01/2021. 1) Please explain and provide the legal authority/legal basis for distribution to Kevin Murphy as successor trustee of the Brian Joseph Callewart Irrevocable Special Needs Trust. It appears the interest may have vested in Brian Callewaert as an individual. An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 11/15/2021 [LRSF 14.7 B.] Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF CORA MAE WILLIAMS
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION, Appointment Under IAEA
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 12/7/21. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: No courtesy copy other than of the petition has been provided. [LRSF 2.7 B. and 14.7 B. and C.] Notwithstanding the local rules, the supporting papers were reviewed online as a ONE-TIME COURTESY. Only the first page of the Duties and Liabilities appears to have been filed. (Only the first page was scanned.) No proof of publication has been filed. The Confidential Statement of Birth Date and Driver's License Number (Judicial Council Form DE-147S) is also required, pursuant to Probate Code Section 8404(b). Form DE-147S must be filed separately and confidentially. When second generation or more distant heirs are listed, the deceased ancestor(s) through whom they take must be named, the relationship(s) to the decedent stated, and the date(s) of death specified. [LRSF 14.18 C.] Whose children are the two grandsons? (If a decedent was survived by issue, her siblings and their issue are not heirs-at-law.) Did Sherryl Labrenda Harris die intestate? Who are her successors-in-interest? If she had a will or trust, who is the executor of her will or trustee of her trust? Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 11/26/21. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.
